campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's financial records for July 2025, including how the city invested its money and the overall treasury status. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, such as better connections between different types of transit.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment transactions and account activity
City treasury financial report for July
Multimodal transportation improvements (buses, biking, walking connections)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council introduced a new ordinance (city law) and decided to fast-track it by skipping the usual first reading process, planning to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and first reading waived
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next council meeting
Expedited approval process used

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, primarily focused on approving official records from previous meetings in March. The council also moved forward with authorizing the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully detailed in the agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of meeting minutes from multiple March 2026 sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager granted signing authority for the construction project

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize and finalize their upcoming meeting agendas. They reviewed what items should be added to the April 21st and April 28th meetings, decided which topics needed specific time slots, and checked legal notice requirements for any agenda items or documents.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 21, 2026 council meeting agenda
Reviewed draft agenda for April 28, 2026 meeting
Set time slots for specific agenda items
Checked public notice and waiver requirements
Reviewed public records from early April 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting where they reviewed the city's spending plans for the upcoming 2026-27 fiscal year, focusing on which improvement projects to fund. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in the city.
keyTopics: 2026-27 budget and capital improvement projects (roads, parks, facilities)
Teen Month recognition and youth initiatives
National Volunteer Week proclamation
City infrastructure and spending priorities

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review their previous meeting notes and discussed changes to tenant protection rules in Sunnyvale. The main focus was introducing a new ordinance that would update how the city handles residential tenant protections.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25 meeting minutes
New ordinance changes to tenant protection program rules
Updates to Chapter 19.71 of city housing code

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 16, 2026 where residents were given time to speak about any city matters for up to 3 minutes each. The council also held elections for chair and vice chair positions and began discussing next year's budget.
keyTopics: Public comment period - residents could address the council on any city issue
Chair and Vice Chair elections for council leadership
Fiscal Year 2027 budget development and presentation

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal notices for Santa Clara residents. If you disagree with a city decision (like a permit denial or zoning ruling), you have 90 days from the decision date to file a lawsuit challenging it, or you lose your right to challenge it in court.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the city over decisions
Legal rights if you disagree with city rulings
Quasi-adjudicative decisions (permits, zoning, etc.)
California Code of Civil Procedure requirements

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to discuss updates to the city's housing and community development plans for 2025-2030, review new project requests from council members, and consider changes to how the city fills council vacancies. The council also approved meeting minutes and previewed upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Update to federal housing and community development funding plan (Consolidated Plan)
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Changes to the council vacancy filling process
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner wants to officially recognize the building as historically important and is asking for permission to add 5,000 square feet of extra floor space if they fix up the building and make it safer against earthquakes.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade (Category 4 to Category 2)
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for seismic and historic rehabilitation
Downtown Commercial zone development
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
